name: GHK-Cu
subtitle: Copper Peptide | Skin Regeneration & Anti-Aging Compound

modifications: Complexed with copper (Cu2+) for enhanced bioavailability

overview: Naturally occurring copper tripeptide found in human plasma, saliva, and urine. GHK-Cu affects 31.2% of human genes and declines significantly with age—from ~200 ng/ml at age 20 to ~80 ng/ml at age 60. Known for exceptional skin regeneration, wound healing, and anti-aging properties.
mechanism: Penetrates skin layers, activates fibroblasts, stimulates collagen and elastin synthesis, promotes angiogenesis, and modulates inflammatory responses. The copper ion is essential for enzyme cofactor activity in tissue repair processes.
keyBenefits: Enhanced collagen production (70% increase in type I and III)

items.name: Collagen Synthesis
items.description: Increases type I and III collagen production by up to 70% in human dermal fibroblasts.

items.name: Elastin Production
items.description: Stimulates elastin synthesis for improved skin elasticity and resilience.

items.name: Skin Barrier Function
items.description: Strengthens protective barrier and improves moisture retention.

items.name: Follicle Stimulation
items.description: Promotes hair follicle enlargement and extends anagen (growth) phase.

items.name: Scalp Health
items.description: Improves scalp circulation and reduces inflammation.

items.name: Tissue Protection
items.description: Protects tissues from oxidative stress and damage.

deliveryMethods.overview: Most common delivery method for skin and hair applications. Available as creams, serums, and solutions.

steps: Cleanse skin thoroughly with gentle cleanser
Pat skin dry and wait 5 minutes
Apply thin, even layer to target areas
Massage gently until fully absorbed (1-2 minutes)
Follow with moisturizer if using serum
Always apply sunscreen during daytime use

deliveryMethods.overview: Injectable form provides systemic effects but is not FDA-approved. Requires refrigeration after reconstitution.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature (15-20 minutes)
Clean vial tops with alcohol swabs
Draw calculated BAC water into syringe
Gently swirl until fully dissolved (light blue color is normal)
Store reconstituted solution at 2-8°C, use within 30 days

interactions.notes: Complementary tissue healing and regeneration pathways.
Enhanced wound healing and tissue repair when combined.
Both affect melanin production; use caution when combining.
Risk of copper toxicity; do not combine with oral copper supplements.
May affect copper absorption and activity.

timeline.effects: Reduction in fine lines and increased skin smoothness
Visible improvement in skin tone and clarity

stopSigns: Severe skin irritation, burning, or persistent redness
Worsening of skin condition after 4-6 weeks
contraindications: Known copper sensitivity or Wilson's disease
Active skin infections at application site
monitoring: Avoid contact with eyes and mucous membranes

good: Light blue tint from copper content
Stable formulation without separation or crystallization
warning: Slight color variation acceptable within blue spectrum

references.title: Regenerative and Protective Actions of GHK-Cu Peptide
references.keyFindings: Demonstrates broad regenerative potential across multiple tissue types including skin, hair, and bone.

references.title: GHK Peptide in Skin Regeneration
references.keyFindings: GHK-Cu increased elastin and collagen production in human dermal fibroblasts by up to 70%.

references.title: GHK-Cu in Prevention of Oxidative Stress and Aging
references.keyFindings: Shows neuroprotective and antioxidant effects in aged mice at 10 mg/kg dosing.
